text: The patient is positioned in a prone jack-knife position. The surgeon performs a dorsal perineal block by injecting a mixture of lidocaine and bupivacaine into three directions at each site. An Onsler anoscope is inserted into the anal canal.

The surgeon retracts the hemorrhoidal tissue at the three o'clock position into the lumen using forceps and performs a V-shaped excision extending two centimeters above the dentate line. Continuous sutures are applied to ligate the vascular pedicle up to the perineal skin. The same procedure is repeated at the eleven o'clock position, followed by coagulation and suturing of the hemorrhoidal tissue at the seven o'clock position.
The surgeon places a hemostatic sponge intra-anally to control bleeding within the rectal cavity.
